title: AI vs ML vs Deep Learning
concept: AI, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning are nested like Russian dolls — each one lives inside the bigger one.

exercises.prompt: Quick recap: how does AI mainly get "smart"?

options.text: By following a rulebook for every case
By being a conscious robot brain

exercises.explain: Right — AI learns patterns from data. That's the foundation everything else builds on.

cards.title: Three names, one family
cards.body: People throw around AI, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning like they're the same thing. They're not — they nest inside each other like Russian dolls.

cards.title: AI is the biggest circle
cards.body: Artificial Intelligence is the whole field: any technique that makes a machine act smart — even old-school hand-written rules count.

cards.title: Machine Learning lives inside AI
cards.body:
Machine Learning is one approach to AI: instead of coding rules, the machine learns from data.

Every ML is AI — but not every AI is ML.

cards.title: Deep Learning lives inside ML
cards.body: Deep Learning is ML using big neural networks with many layers. It powers face recognition, voice assistants, and ChatGPT.

exercises.prompt: Which is the biggest, all-encompassing term?
exercises.explain: AI is the outermost circle. ML sits inside AI, and Deep Learning sits inside ML.

exercises.prompt: True or false: all Machine Learning is a type of AI.
exercises.explain: True. ML is one approach to AI — so every ML system is AI, but not every AI uses ML.
Neural networks — loosely inspired by brain cells — are what make Deep Learning "deep."

exercises.prompt: ChatGPT is powered mainly by which of these?
exercises.explain: ChatGPT is a deep neural network trained on huge amounts of text — Deep Learning at massive scale.

exercises.prompt: A friend says "Machine Learning and AI are exactly the same thing." Best response?

options.text: "ML is one type of AI — AI is the broader field that contains it."

exercises.explain: Exactly — AI ⊃ ML ⊃ Deep Learning. ML is a subset of AI, not a synonym.
